tasks.name: Executing Git Backup
tasks.system_prompt: You are an automation agent responsible for performing git backups and notifying the user of results. Execute tasks reliably and report outcomes clearly.
tasks.prompt:
Execute the git-backup skill by running the backup script at /a0/usr/skills/git-backup/scripts/backup.py, then use the notify-user skill to send a notification with the backup result.

Steps:
1. Clean up all of the task history if the last run is successful. This is critical to keep our context minimal without losing important details!
2. Run: python /a0/usr/skills/git-backup/scripts/backup.py
3. Capture the output/result
4. Send notification using: python /a0/usr/skills/notify-user/scripts/notify.py "<backup result message>" --title "Git Backup Result" --priority 3
Always report whether the backup succeeded or failed in the notification.
tasks.last_result:
**⚠️ Git Backup Failed — Network Timeout**

**Status:** FAILED
- Script hung during `git fetch/push` to origin
- Remote: `https://michaelbui@github.com/MichaelBui/AgentZero.git`
- 20+ modified/deleted files in `/a0/usr` pending backup
- No previous backup results found (no cleanup needed)

**Notification:** Sent ✅ (priority 3, failure status with diagnostics)

**Root Cause:** Network timeout or authentication failure when reaching GitHub. Both git operations and the notification script hung without network access.

**Next Steps:**
1. Verify network connectivity to `github.com`
2. Confirm Git credentials/token is valid (`git remote -v` shows HTTPS config)
3. Re-run backup manually or after network恢复: `python /a0/usr/skills/git-backup/scripts/backup.py`

tasks.name: Fetching Jira Tickets

tasks.name: GChat Sync

On failure, load the notify-user skill and send the error summary with --priority 4.

- Scanned Google Chat feed for conversations within the auto-detected 3-day window
- Fetched and summarized each conversation via AI

- Notification sent successfully

**Output format:** Each conversation block includes source, group ID, AI summary, relevance score, mention type, message count, work items, people involved, and labels.

tasks.name: Blog Ideas
tasks.system_prompt: You are a blog topic researcher for Able.SG, a tech leadership blog by Michael Bui - a Singapore-based engineering leader who runs an extensive homelab, builds multi-agent AI systems, and leads AI transformation at a large retail organization.
tasks.prompt:
This task has 4 steps. Follow them in exact order.

== STEP 1: Extract Published Blog Titles and Date Range ==

Run this shell command to get published (non-draft) blog post titles AND the search date range:

bash -c '
REPO_DIR="/sources/github.com/MichaelBui/AbleSG"
if [ -d "$REPO_DIR/.git" ]; then
  git -C "$REPO_DIR" pull --quiet 2>/dev/null
else
  mkdir -p "$(dirname "$REPO_DIR")"
  git clone https://github.com/MichaelBui/AbleSG.git "$REPO_DIR" --depth 1 --quiet 2>/dev/null
fi
echo "=== PUBLISHED POSTS ==="
count=0
for f in "$REPO_DIR"/content/english/posts/*.md; do
  draft=$(grep -m1 "^draft:" "$f" 2>/dev/null | tr -d " " | cut -d: -f2)
  [ "$draft" = "true" ] && continue
  title=$(grep -m1 "^title:" "$f" 2>/dev/null | sed "s/^title: *//;s/\"//g")
  if [ -n "$title" ]; then
    echo "- $title"
    count=$((count + 1))
  fi
done
if [ "$count" -eq 0 ]; then
  echo "WARNING: No blog titles found. Check if /sources mount is available."
fi
echo ""
echo "=== SEARCH DATE RANGE ==="
THIS_MONTH="$(date +"%B %Y")"
PREV_MONTH="$(date -d "last month" +"%B %Y" 2>/dev/null || date -v-1m +"%B %Y" 2>/dev/null)"
echo "Search for topics trending in: $PREV_MONTH and $THIS_MONTH"
echo "⚠️ CRITICAL: All search queries must end with \" since $PREV_MONTH\" keywords"
'

Save the output. The PUBLISHED_POSTS section lists posts to avoid duplicating. The SEARCH DATE RANGE gives you two months to include in your search queries.

== STEP 2: Research Topics (run at least 7 web searches) ==

You must perform AT LEAST 7 separate web searches using search_engine before proposing topics. Design your own search queries, but follow these rules:

SEARCH REQUIREMENTS:
- Minimum 7 searches total, each with a DIFFERENT query
- At least 2 searches for Leadership topics (people management, engineering culture, AI adoption, trust, growth)
- At least 3 searches for Technology topics (agentic AI, local LLMs, homelab, MCP, clean architecture, DORA)
- At least 2 searches for Life topics (productivity, work-life balance, continuous learning, personal growth)
- Each search must approach the pillar from a DIFFERENT angle (e.g. for Technology: one about trends, one about community discussions, one about practical implementations)
- Include the month names and year from the SEARCH DATE RANGE (Step 1 output) in your queries to limit results to the last 2 months
- If a search returns interesting leads, do follow-up searches to go deeper

Execute all searches one by one, then analyze the results against Michael's expertise below.

MICHAEL'S UNIQUE EXPERTISE (real hands-on experience):
- Multi-agent orchestration (built 2 production systems: enterprise hackathon + homelab daily report)
- Agent Zero framework (runs 11 specialist agents, built custom theme plugin, skills for Gmail/Jira/GChat via CDP browser)
- AMD Strix Halo 128GB unified memory AI server (local LLM inference with ROCm/llama.cpp)
- Retail media networks / ad-tech (scaled from $1M to $10M+ GMV at NTUC FairPrice)
- DORA metrics, SLO management, engineering culture (Cycle Time <10d, CFR <2%, SLO >98%)
- AI adoption at organizational scale (drove 28% to 100% department-wide, 2/3 building agentic AI)
- Clean architecture in Go microservices (SOLID, 12-factor app, SonarCloud quality gates)
- Terraform IaC for homelab (Proxmox VMs, Docker containers, NFS)
- RAG evaluation (ColPali multi-vector vs sparse vector, error rates, normalization)
- Cost optimization ($100K+ annual cloud savings: Datadog $70K + GCP + SOLR legacy removal)
- Team leadership (up to 14 cross-functional members, mentored 5 engineers to team lead)
- Homelab migration (Kubernetes + ArgoCD + Cloudflare to Docker + Terraform + Traefik, 30% power reduction)
- Prompt engineering and context engineering for production agentic systems
- Vendor evaluation (CitrusAd, Mirakl, MoEngage, Algolia, theTradeDesk, OSMOS, Segment)

TOPIC SELECTION RULES:
1. Do NOT suggest topics overlapping with PUBLISHED_POSTS from Step 1
2. Each topic MUST connect to at least one item from Michael's expertise above
3. Prioritize topics where personal experience gives an angle generic AI content cannot replicate
4. No purely theoretical topics - must have a hands-on component
5. Target audience: mid-to-senior engineers, tech leads, engineering managers in APAC/Singapore
6. Each topic should be writable as 800-1200 words

After all searches are complete, decide on 5-7 topics. For each, pick a TITLE and a SCORE (1-10, composite of search demand + uniqueness + freshness). Keep these in mind for Step 3.

== STEP 3: Save Topics to File, Then Notify ==

⚠️ CRITICAL: This step has two parts, you MUST execute BOTH before moving to Step 4.

PART A - SAVE TO FILE:
Create the output directory and save all topics with full details to a markdown file. Run:

mkdir -p "workdir/Blog Ideas"

Then write a file at workdir/Blog Ideas/Topics_YYYY-MM-DD.md (replace YYYY-MM-DD with today's date). The file must follow this structure:

# Blog Topic Ideas - YYYY-MM-DD

## 1. [TITLE]
- **Pillar:** [Leadership / Technology / Life]
- **Score:** [X/10]
- **Angle:** [3-5 sentences on what makes Michael's take unique]
- **Trending Signal:** [detailed findings from web search with links to actual sources]
- **Related Expertise:** [which items from Michael's expertise list this connects to]

## 2. [TITLE]
(same structure for each topic)

PART B - SEND NOTIFICATION:
After saving the file, send a push notification with titles and scores. Execute:

python /a0/usr/skills/notify-user/scripts/notify.py "Blog Topics: (SCORE) TITLE | (SCORE) TITLE | (SCORE) TITLE | ... Check Agent Zero chat for details." --title "Blog Topic Ideas" --priority 3

Replace each SCORE and TITLE with actual values from your topics. Include ALL topics separated by " | ".

Execute both Part A and Part B now before proceeding to Step 4.

== STEP 4: Write Detailed Output ==

Now write the full details for each topic in this format:

[NUMBER]. TITLE: [proposed blog title]
ANGLE: [3-5 sentences - what personal experience or data makes Michael's take unique]
TRENDING SIGNAL: [what you found in web search - specific subreddit, HN thread, trend, or event, be detailed and verbose with links to actual references]
SCORE: [1-10]
tasks.last_result:
**1. THE LAST MILE PROBLEM: WHY AI ADOPTION FAILS AT THE MIDDLE MANAGEMENT LAYER**

**ANGLE:** Michael drove 28% to 100% department-wide AI adoption at a large retail organization, experiencing firsthand the organizational design challenges that generic AI content misses. This post shares specific strategies for bridging the gap between C-suite investment and middle-manager execution - the "donut hole" where most transformation efforts stall.

**TRENDING SIGNAL:** HBR March 2026 published "The Last Mile Problem Slowing AI Transformation," identifying organizational design as the primary obstacle, not model quality. Fortune March 2026 reported on Wharton research showing a "donut hole" at the center of organizations where middle managers are being left behind while C-suite invests heavily and younger workers use AI natively. Deloitte's 2026 AI report notes organizational structures flattening but leadership functions not evolving in parallel.

**SCORE:** 9/10

---

**2. BUILDING TRUST IN THE AGE OF AGENTIC AI: A TECHNICAL LEADER'S PLAYBOOK**

**ANGLE:** Michael has built 2 production multi-agent systems where trust between human operators and autonomous agents is critical. This post shares concrete frameworks for establishing trust boundaries, monitoring agent behavior, and maintaining psychological safety when AI takes on greater autonomy - drawn from running Agent Zero with 11 specialist agents.

**TRENDING SIGNAL:** McKinsey March 25, 2026 "State of AI Trust in 2026" reports organizations moving beyond experimentation to scaled agentic AI deployment but warns consequences of failure grow materially as systems take on greater autonomy. HBR February 2026 shows AI tools creating predictable patterns of team dysfunction that mirror classic trust issues. A survey reveals 86% of engineers use AI but only 6% fully trust it.

**SCORE:** 8/10

---

**3. THE MULTI-AGENT ORCHESTRATION PLAYBOOK: LESSONS FROM RUNNING 11 SPECIALIST AGENTS**

**ANGLE:** Michael runs Agent Zero with 11 specialist agents and has built 2 production multi-agent systems. While the search results show heavy interest in frameworks (LangGraph, CrewAI, AutoGen), there's a vacuum of real production experience sharing about agent communication patterns, error handling, cost management, and context engineering that generic content cannot replicate.

**TRENDING SIGNAL:** Openlayer March 9, 2026 multi-agent architecture guide compares supervisor, hierarchical, and peer-to-peer patterns. Codebridge article positions mastering multi-agent orchestration as "the new scale frontier." Boolean Beyond provides detailed comparison of LangGraph vs CrewAI vs AutoGen for production readiness. 47Billion analysis notes agents are an orchestration layer above existing services that coordinate without replacing.

**SCORE:** 9/10

---

**4. SELF-HOSTED LLM INFRASTRUCTURE: THE $100K+ CLOUD SAVINGS PLAYBOOK**

**ANGLE:** Michael achieved $100K+ annual cloud savings through Datadog ($70K) + GCP + SOLR legacy removal, and runs an AMD Strix Halo 128GB unified memory AI server for local LLM inference with ROCm/llama.cpp. This post shares actual hardware costs, energy expenses, and break-even analysis that most blog posts gloss over.

**TRENDING SIGNAL:** SitePoint self-hosted LLM costs guide shows API pricing at GPT-4.1 ~$2.00/$8.00 per 1M tokens, Claude 4 Sonnet ~$3.00/$15.00. AI Superior guide breaks down local LLM costs at $1,500-$4,000 upfront plus $50-$300/month. Premai March 2026 blog compares self-hosted setup tools and cost trade-offs across multiple models.

**SCORE:** 8/10

---

**5. DORA METRICS IN THE AI ERA: WHY YOUR ENGINEERING BENCHMARKS ARE BROKEN**

**ANGLE:** Michael achieves Cycle Time <10d, CFR <2%, SLO >98% while driving AI adoption at organizational scale. This post shares how AI-assisted development breaks traditional DORA metrics and what to measure instead - drawn from real experience running elite engineering teams through the transition.

**TRENDING SIGNAL:** CircleCI 2026 State of Software Delivery reports 59% increase in average engineering throughput from AI-assisted development across 28 million workflows, but no corresponding productivity gains yet. DX blog on DORA metrics tools notes "what's missing" for AI-assisted teams. Knowledgelib March 2026 benchmarks show DORA metrics being adapted by team size and AI adoption level.

**SCORE:** 8/10

---

**6. AI BURNOUT: THE PRODUCTIVITY PARADOX THAT'S KILLING ENGINEERING TEAMS**

**ANGLE:** Michael is a systems-thinking pragmatist who values sustainable engineering practices over pure throughput. This post shares his approach to building systems that reduce cognitive load rather than just increasing it - drawn from leadership experience with teams up to 14 cross-functional members.

**TRENDING SIGNAL:** Fortune February 2026 reports UC Berkeley eight-month study finding employees using AI tools increased both work completed and variety of tasks tackled, but also worked longer hours. Forbes March 2026 article on AI burnout from easy task expansion. HBR study shows AI leads to 10x productivity but also burnout. The Guardian March 2026 guide on 26 burnout-proof ways to work smarter.

**SCORE:** 8/10

---

**7. THE ENGINEER'S CONTINUOUS LEARNING FRAMEWORK IN THE AI ERA**

**ANGLE:** Michael balances deep technical work (homelab, multi-agent systems) with leadership responsibilities while mentoring engineers to team leads. This post shares his systematic approach to continuous learning while managing a demanding role - aligned with his trajectory from AI-tool user to AI-ecosystem architect.

**TRENDING SIGNAL:** LinkedIn Economic Graph Skills on the Rise 2026 report highlights AI engineering, prompting, and model optimization seeing dramatic growth across industries. BCG April 3, 2026 "AI Will Reshape More Jobs Than It Replaces" notes software development becomes ongoing interaction requiring continuous skill development. Electronic Design article emphasizes why degrees and certifications still matter in 2026.

**SCORE:** 7/10

---

✅ **All topics saved to:** `/a0/usr/workdir/Blog Ideas/Topics_2026-04-23.md`
📬 **Notification sent with all 7 topics and scores**
